raw_consumer_statement: XXXX charged my American Express on XX/XX/year> for a car rental I did NOT authorize. I filed XXXX disputes with XXXX. XXXX was soon after I discovered the unauthorized charge. XXXX closed my account with an invoice supplied by the vendor. I had to open the dispute again, pointing out the problem with the vendor invoice -- no signature from me authorizing the charge. XXXX closed the dispute without notifying me and without giving me a reason. Third time, XXXX again closed the dispute carelessly, citing the vendor 's invoice did not show " over charge '', which was NOT what I disputed. I showed them clearly the vendor did not have my authorizing signature for the charge. Not only XXXX carelessly closed the dispute without reading my statement and ignored the fact the vendor invoice is missing my signature. Completely breached their contract with the customers that they will take actions protecting customers from fraudulent charges. The reasons XXXX cites to deem the charge valid are only based on assumption, not substantiated with evidence and have nothing to do with the fact I was not at vendor 's counter on XX/XX/year>, I did not agree to the charge ( no signature ) and I did not rent a car on XX/XX/XXXX.
